subject: ap-calculus-bc
theme: Differential equations model real-world growth and change — BC extends AB with Euler's method for numerical approximation and the logistic model for bounded growth.

core_concepts.title: Separation of Variables
core_concepts.detail: Rewrite $\frac{dy}{dx} = f(x)g(y)$ as $\frac{1}{g(y)}\,dy = f(x)\,dx$, then integrate both sides. Include $+C$ on one side and solve for $y$ if possible.

core_concepts.title: Slope Fields
core_concepts.detail: A slope field shows $\frac{dy}{dx}$ at many points as short line segments. Solution curves follow the field; horizontal segments indicate $\frac{dy}{dx} = 0$.

core_concepts.title: Exponential Growth and Decay
core_concepts.detail: $\frac{dy}{dt} = ky$ has solution $y = Ce^{kt}$. If $k > 0$, the quantity grows exponentially; if $k < 0$, it decays. The initial condition $y(0) = y_0$ gives $C = y_0$.

core_concepts.title: Euler's Method (BC)
core_concepts.detail: Numerical approximation: $y_{n+1} = y_n + f(x_n, y_n) \cdot \Delta x$. Start from an initial condition and step forward by $\Delta x$. Smaller steps give better accuracy.

core_concepts.title: Logistic Growth (BC)
core_concepts.detail: $\frac{dP}{dt} = kP\left(1 - \frac{P}{L}\right)$ models bounded growth with carrying capacity $L$. Growth is fastest at $P = \frac{L}{2}$. Solution: $P(t) = \frac{L}{1 + Ae^{-kt}}$.

core_concepts.title: Equilibrium Solutions
core_concepts.detail: Values of $y$ where $\frac{dy}{dx} = 0$ for all $x$. These are constant solutions (horizontal lines). In logistic models, $P = 0$ and $P = L$ are equilibria.

formulas.name: Exponential Growth/Decay
formulas.description: The simplest differential equation model. k > 0 is growth, k < 0 is decay. C is determined by the initial condition.
formulas.example: A population doubles every 3 hours: $y = y_0 e^{kt}$, $2y_0 = y_0 e^{3k}$, so $k = \frac{\ln 2}{3}$.

formulas.name: Euler's Method
formulas.description: BC-only numerical method. Uses the slope at the current point to estimate the next y-value. Each step adds the slope times the step size.

formulas.name: Logistic Differential Equation
formulas.description: BC-only model for growth with a carrying capacity L. Growth slows as P approaches L. Maximum growth rate occurs at P = L/2.

formulas.name: Logistic Solution
formulas.description: Closed-form solution to the logistic equation. A is determined by the initial population P₀. As t → ∞, P → L.

exam_tip: On Euler's method FRQs, show each step's computation clearly — the AP grader checks intermediate values. For logistic models, the inflection point (fastest growth) always occurs at $P = \frac{L}{2}$.
